selfAssessment.revisionLevel: I write in messy drafts, then read the essay out loud and cut the lines that sound fake
selfAssessment.evidenceApproach: I do not think in terms of formal evidence; I build around scenes, repeated images, and small objects that can carry meaning
selfAssessment.conclusionApproach: I usually end by circling back to an image or sound instead of stating a clean lesson
selfAssessment.wordCountTendency: I usually land close to the target, but I overwrite certain passages when I get attached to an image
writingHabits: I start paragraphs with a sensory detail before I explain why it matters
I struggle with scene pacing and sometimes summarize too fast between vivid moments
I overuse metaphor when I get nervous
I like wistful endings even when the piece might need a sharper stop
quoteIntroStyle: In the craft essay we read
One line from workshop that stuck with me was
selfEditFocus: cutting the prettiest line if it is doing no work
making sure scenes are actually on the page
trimming metaphors that feel too decorated
selfAssessment.timeSpentOn: openings, paragraph rhythm, and the last few lines
